revision.comment: 難波八阪神社(なんばやさかじんじゃ)とは大阪府大阪市の神社（[[commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]]より初版）
肖像権の問題のない画像に差し替え

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=難波八阪神社| 所在地=大阪府大阪市浪速区元町2-9-19| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|}}| 祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[奇稲田姫命]]、[[八柱御子命]]など。| 社格=| 創建=不詳| 例祭=| 本殿=| 神事=| 画像=[[Image:Namba-yasaka-jinja_haiden1.jpg|250px]]}} '''難波八阪神社'''(なんばやさかじんじゃ)とは[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[奇稲田姫命]]、[[八柱御子命]]など。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。
獅子舞台という大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 ==摂社・末社== *篠山神社（地元の代官・篠山十兵衛景義が祭られている） *稲荷神社 *三宝荒神社 *市杵島姫神社 *皇大神社
== 祭礼 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12～14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 <gallery> Image:Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 </gallery>
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:浪速区]]

{{神社| 名称=難波八阪神社| 所在地=大阪府大阪市浪速区元町2-9-19| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|}}| 祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[奇稲田姫命]]、[[八柱御子命]]など。| 社格=| 創建=不詳| 例祭=| 本殿=| 神事=| 画像=[[Image:Namba-yasaka-jinja_haiden1.jpg|250px]]}} '''難波八阪神社'''(なんばやさかじんじゃ)とは[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[奇稲田姫命]]、[[八柱御子命]]など。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。
獅子舞台という大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 ==摂社・末社== *篠山神社（地元の代官・篠山十兵衛景義が祭られている） *稲荷神社 *三宝荒神社 *市杵島姫神社 *皇大神社

== 祭礼 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 *綱引神事祭 1月第3日曜日 <gallery> File:Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 File:Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 </gallery>

{{神社| 名称=難波八阪神社| 所在地=大阪府大阪市浪速区元町2-9-19| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}| 祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[八柱御子命]]など。| 社格=| 創建=不詳| 例祭=| 本殿=| 別名=|難波下社 神事=| 画像=[[File:Namba-yasaka-jinja_haiden1.jpg|250px]]}} '''難波八阪神社'''(なんばやさかじんじゃ)とは[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[八柱御子命]]など。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。
== 概要 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病がはやりだしたところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。
それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]]～[[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。
[[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]([[明治]]5年)に郷社となった。現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年（昭和49年）5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。
==摂社・末社== *篠山神社（地元の代官・篠山十兵衛景義が祭られている） *[[稲荷神社]] *[[三宝荒神社]] *市杵島姫神社 *皇大神社
== 祭礼 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 *綱引神事祭 1月第3日曜日 <gallery> File:Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 File:Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 </gallery> ==関連項目== *[[難波神社]] 別名は難波上社。 *[[生國魂神社]] 別名は難波大社。
==外部リンク== *[http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]]

それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]]～[[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。
[[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]([[明治]]5年)に郷社となった。現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年（昭和49年）5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。

== 概要 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病がはやりだしたところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。
それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]]～[[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。
[[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]([[明治]]5年)に郷社となった。現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年（昭和49年）5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。
==摂社・末社== *篠山神社（地元の代官・篠山十兵衛景義が祭られている） *[[稲荷神社]] *[[三宝荒神社]] *市杵島姫神社 *皇大神社
== 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 * 綱引神事祭 1月第3日曜日

'''難波八阪神社'''(なんばやさかじんじゃ)とは[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[八柱御子命]]など。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。
== 概要 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病がはやりだしたところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。
それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]]～[[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。
[[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]([[明治]]5年)に郷社となった。現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年（昭和49年）5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。
==摂社・末社== *篠山神社（地元の代官・篠山十兵衛景義が祭られている） *[[稲荷神社]] *[[三宝荒神社]] *市杵島姫神社 *皇大神社
== 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 * 綱引神事祭 1月第3日曜日

{{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]]

{{神社 | 名称=難波八阪神社 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9-19 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} | 祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[仁徳天皇]]の時代 | 例祭=7月12 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） | 本殿= | 別名=難波下社 | 神事= | 画像=[[File:Namba-yasaka-jinja_haiden1.jpg|250px]] }} '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。
==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病がはやりだしたところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。
それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。
[[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。
現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]]
== 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 <gallery> File:Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 File:Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 </gallery>
== 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。
== 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 * 綱引神事祭 - 1月第3日曜日
==関連項目== {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} *[[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 *[[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。
==外部リンク== *[http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト]

== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]]
== 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 <gallery> Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 </gallery>
== 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。

revision.comment: took from current article [[Nanba Shrine]] https://simple.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Nanba_Shrine&oldid=9095976 since it might be moved to [[Nanba Yasaka Shrine]]
revision.text:
{{one source|date=May 2023}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Nanba Shrine honsha.jpg|deity=[[Emperor Nintoku]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
all the [[kami]] of [[Settsu Province]] in its [[Soja shrine]]|type=[[Soja shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} Some people think it was the [[Soja shrine]] of [[Settsu Province]]. Soja shrines are shrines where all the [[kami]] of a province can be worshipped at once. == References == {{Reflist}} {{religion-stub}} [[:Category:Gion shrines]] [[:Category:Soja shrines]] [[:Category:Ken-sha]] [[:Category:Osaka]] [[:Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]]

{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社[[ja:難波八阪神社]]) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974 {{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. . The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »], ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. . == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »], ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka {{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. . ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} [[en:draft:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] }} [[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]]

Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974 {{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka {{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. . ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] }} [[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]]

{{混同|難波神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 難波八阪神社 |画像 = [[画像:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9-19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[仁徳天皇]]の時代 |例祭 = 7月12 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） |本殿 = |別名 = 難波下社 |神事 = }} '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。 == 歴史 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病が流行り出したところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。 それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。 [[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。 現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 == 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。 == 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 * 綱引神事祭 - 1月第3日曜日 == 関連項目 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 * [[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。 == 外部リンク == * [http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]]

revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »], ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »], ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] }} [[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]]

The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] }} [[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]]

revision.text:
{{混同|難波神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 難波八阪神社 |画像 = [[画像:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9-19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[仁徳天皇]]の時代 |例祭 = 7月12 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） |本殿 = |別名 = 難波下社 |神事 = }} '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。 == 歴史 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病が流行り出したところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。 それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。 [[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。 現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 == 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。 == 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 * 綱引神事祭 - 1月第3日曜日 == 関連項目 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 * [[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。 == 外部リンク == * [http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]]

{{混同|難波神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 難波八阪神社 |画像 = [[画像:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|type=point}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9-19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[仁徳天皇]]の時代 |例祭 = 7月12 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） |本殿 = |別名 = 難波下社 |神事 = }} '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。 == 歴史 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病が流行り出したところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。 それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。 [[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。 現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 == 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。 == 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 * 綱引神事祭 - 1月第3日曜日 == 関連項目 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 * [[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。 == 外部リンク == * [http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]]

{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|type=point}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9-19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[仁徳天皇]]の時代 |例祭 = 7月12 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） |本殿 = |別名 = 難波下社 |神事 = }} '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。 == 歴史 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病が流行り出したところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。 それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。 [[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。 現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 == 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。 == 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御 * 綱引神事祭 - 1月第3日曜日 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 * [[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。 == 外部リンク == * [http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]]

revision.comment: /* 祭礼 */ 「難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭）7月12 - 14日 12日船渡御・13日宵宮・14日本宮陸渡御」→「難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭）：7月12日 - 14日（12日船渡御、13日宵宮、14日本宮陸渡御）」に修正。他。

{{混同|難波神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 難波八阪神社 |画像 = [[画像:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|type=point}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9-19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[仁徳天皇]]の時代 |例祭 = 7月12 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） |本殿 = |別名 = 難波下社 |神事 = }} '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。 == 歴史 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病が流行り出したところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。 それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。 [[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。 現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 == 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。 == 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）：7月12日 - 14日（12日[[船渡御]]、13日宵宮、14日本宮[[神幸祭|陸渡御]]） * 綱引神事祭：1月第3日曜日 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 * [[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。 == 外部リンク == * [http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]]

{{混同|難波神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 難波八阪神社 |画像 = [[画像:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */ [[Wikipedia:スタイルマニュアル/見出し#マークアップについて]]「見出しの直前に複数行の空行を入れると、空白が大きくなるので入れないようにしてください。」に基づき、「外部リンク」節の直後に追加された空行を除去。

拝殿
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|type=point}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9番地19号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] |社格 = 旧・[[郷社]] |創建 = [[仁徳天皇]]の[[時代]]（[[古墳時代#空白の4世紀|伝承の時代]]） |例祭 = 7月12 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） |本殿 = |別名 = 難波下社 |神事 = }} [[File:Goka ni karahana ni hidarimitsudomoe（osaka nanbayasakajinja）.png|250px|thumb|難波八阪神社の[[家紋#神紋・寺紋|神紋]]。]] '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧・[[社格#近代社格制度|社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。 == 歴史 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病が流行り出したところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたので、これを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。 それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。 [[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。 現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年（昭和49年）5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の[[代官]]） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 == 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。 == 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）：7月12日 - 14日（12日[[船渡御]]、13日宵宮、14日本宮[[神幸祭|陸渡御]]） * 綱引神事祭：1月第3日曜日 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 * [[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。 == 外部リンク == * [http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社　公式サイト] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]]

{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] }}

Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] }}

{{混同|難波神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 難波八阪神社 |画像 = [[画像:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|type=point}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9-19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[仁徳天皇]]の時代 |例祭 = 7月12 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） |本殿 = |別名 = 難波下社 |神事 = }} '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。 == 歴史 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病が流行り出したところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。 それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。 [[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。 現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 == 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。 == 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）：7月12日 - 14日（12日[[船渡御]]、13日宵宮、14日本宮[[神幸祭|陸渡御]]） * 綱引神事祭：1月第3日曜日 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 * [[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。 == 外部リンク == * [http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]]

{{混同|難波神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 難波八阪神社 |画像 = [[画像:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿

revision.comment: テンプレート内の例祭の記載「7月12 - 14日」を、本文の祭礼の記載「7月12日 - 14日」に統一・修正。

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Original pages]] to [[Category:original pages]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] }}

{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]

Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[category:original pages]] }}

revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] }}

Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[category:dd]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[category:dd]] }}

{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}}{{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[Category:Osaka]] It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社}} == [[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.
