revision.comment: 45 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:難波八阪神社 up to rev 102911402
revision.text:
{{混同|難波神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 難波八阪神社 |画像 = [[画像:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|type=point}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]][[元町 (大阪市)|元町]]2丁目9-19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[仁徳天皇]]の時代 |例祭 = 7月12日 - 14日・難波祇園祭（夏祭・難波祭） |本殿 = |別名 = 難波下社 |神事 = }} [[File:Goka ni karahana ni hidarimitsudomoe（osaka nanbayasakajinja）.png|250px|thumb|難波八阪神社の[[家紋#神紋・寺紋|神紋]]。]] '''難波八阪神社'''（なんばやさかじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[浪速区]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。よく「難波八'''坂'''神社」と書かれているが、これは誤字である。 == 歴史 == [[仁徳天皇]]の時代、この付近一帯に疫病が流行り出したところ、[[牛頭天王]]が現れたのでこれを祀ったのが当社の始まりとされる。 それ以来「難波下宮」と呼ばれており、[[難波]]一帯の[[産土神]]となった。[[延久]]年間（[[1069年]] - [[1073年]]）の頃には牛頭天王を祀った神社として有名であり、[[神宮寺]]の他十二坊の塔頭が並んでいた。 [[明治維新]]後の、[[神仏分離]]により寺は廃絶し、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[郷社]]となった。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）3月13日・14日の第1回[[大阪大空襲]]で社殿が焼失した。 現在の本殿は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）5月に完成した。本殿と同じく、1974年5月に完成した獅子殿という素盞嗚尊の[[荒魂]]を祀る大きな獅子の頭の形をした舞台があることで有名である。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[御子神|八柱御子命]] == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）5月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1974年（昭和49年）5月再建。 * 獅子殿 - 祭神：素盞嗚尊[[荒魂]]。1974年（昭和49年）5月建立。舞台も兼ねている。 * 社務所 * 篠山神社 - 祭神：篠山十兵衛景義（地元の代官） * [[稲荷神社]] * [[三宝荒神]]社 * [[市杵島姫]]神社 * [[皇大神社]] * [[戦艦]][[陸奥 (戦艦)|陸奥]]主砲抑気具記念碑 - 戦艦陸奥の主砲の蓋が設置されている。 Namba-yasaka-jinja shishibutai.jpg|獅子舞台 Gozutenno tsunahiki.jpg|[[摂津名所図会]]「牛頭天王綱引」 == 文化財 == === 大阪市指定無形民俗文化財 === * 綱引神事 - [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）指定（難波八阪神社綱引神事保存会）。 == 祭礼 == * [[難波祇園祭]]（夏祭・難波祭）：7月12日 - 14日（12日[[船渡御]]、13日宵宮、14日本宮[[神幸祭|陸渡御]]） * 綱引神事祭：1月第3日曜日 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{commons|Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} * [[難波神社]] - 別名は難波上社。 * [[生國魂神社]] - 別名は難波大社。 == 外部リンク == * [http://nambayasaka.jp/ 難波八阪神社公式サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なんはやさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:浪速区の歴史]] [[Category:浪速区の建築物]] [[Category:難波]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]]

[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] }} {{translated page|ja|難波八阪神社|version=102911402|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{religion-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nanba Yasaka Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} [[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[simple:user:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[sn:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]] [[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]] [[zh:难波八阪神社]] [[ja:難波八阪神社]] [[sn:anba Yasaka Shrine]]

Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] }} {{translated page|ja|難波八阪神社|version=102911402|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} [[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[simple:user:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[sn:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]] [[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]] [[zh:难波八阪神社]] [[ja:難波八阪神社]] [[sn:anba Yasaka Shrine]]

[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] }} {{translated page|ja|難波八阪神社|version=102911402|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} [[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[simple:user:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[en:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[sn:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]] [[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]] [[zh:难波八阪神社]] [[ja:難波八阪神社]] [[sn:anba Yasaka Shrine]]

Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] }} {{translated The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{coordinates|34|39|40.4|N|135|29|48.1|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=難波八阪神社}}|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]]
[[Yashimajinumi]]|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]}} Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}} It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}} [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]] ==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[:en:Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}. The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. == Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] . [http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018. Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}. ==Gallery== File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji == Related pages == * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}}{{Commons|Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] }} {{translated page|ja|難波八阪神社|version=102911402|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{religion-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja}} [[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[simple:user:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[sn:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]] [[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]] [[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]] [[zh:难波八阪神社]] [[ja:難波八阪神社]] [[sn:anba Yasaka Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11659527]] Wikidata name: Namba-yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.66122222|N|135.49669444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247}}; {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}}|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}}}}
Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[:en:Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659527]] Wikidata name: Namba-yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.66122222|N|135.49669444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}
Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[:en:Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji </gallery>

The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>

Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[:en:Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>

It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659527]] Wikidata name: Namba-yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.66122222|N|135.49669444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}
Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[:en:Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji </gallery>

Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>

In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659527]] Wikidata name: Namba-yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.66122222|N|135.49669444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}
Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of [[:en:Bombing of Osaka|airstrikes on Osaka]], it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659527]] Wikidata name: Namba-yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.66122222|N|135.49669444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}
Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of {{ill|Bombing of Osaka|en|Bombing of Osaka|WD=Q2814395|lt=airstrikes on Osaka}}, it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}} . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|{{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|WD=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya}} File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji </gallery>

Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}} . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]]
{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.66122222|N|135.49669444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}
Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of {{ill|Bombing of Osaka|en|Bombing of Osaka|WD=Q2814395|lt=airstrikes on Osaka}}, it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}} . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|{{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|WD=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya}} File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Namba-yasaka-jinja]][[de:Namba Yasaka-Schrein]][[fr:Namba Yasaka-jinja]][[ja:難波八阪神社]][[simple:Nanba Yasaka Shrine]][[zh:难波八阪神社]]
{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.66122222|N|135.49669444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}
Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of {{ill|Bombing of Osaka|en|Bombing of Osaka|WD=Q2814395|lt=airstrikes on Osaka}}, it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}} . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|{{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|WD=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya}} File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji </gallery>

{{Distinguish|Nanba Shrine}}{{one source|date=May 2023}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Namba-yasaka-jinja haiden1.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.66122222|N|135.49669444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}|type=[[:en:Gion worship|Gion shrine]]|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}}
Nanba Shrine (難波八阪神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}. It is a shrine for [[:en:Gion worship|Gion worship]] dedicated to [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Quin|first=Rebecca|date=2020-01-11|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://travel.gaijinpot.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=GaijinPot Travel|language=en}}</ref>
It has a large lion-shaped building which is famous in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://insideosaka.com/namba-yasaka-shrine/|access-date=2023-05-13|website=Inside Osaka|language=en-US}}</ref> [[File:Namba_Yasaka-jinja.jpg|thumb|Lion head building]]
==History== There is no longer any exact data as to the date of the first construction of this place of worship, but it is mentioned for the first time in the Annals of the Year 1069. Because of {{ill|Bombing of Osaka|en|Bombing of Osaka|WD=Q2814395|lt=airstrikes on Osaka|1=Bombing of Osaka}}, it burned to the ground in 1945. Its reconstruction and its current form date from mai 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Site du Namba Yasaka-jinja|url=http://nambayasaka.jp|access-date=18 juillet 2019|language=ja}}.</ref>
The deity [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (god of protection against epidemics) is honored in this temple.<ref name="Fierce">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan|1=Taiwan}} . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>

== Special features == [[File:Gozutenno_tsunahiki.jpg|thumb|Gozu-tenno Festival (illustration from the end of the XVIII century century ).]] In the central square of the temple there is a stage building 12 meters high, 11 meters wide and 10 meters deep, made of stone, in the shape of a lion's head. It is likely that the lion motif was influenced by the style of temples in {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan|1=Taiwan}} . <ref name="Fierce2">[http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html K. Imai, « Fierce lion head stage emerges as main attraction in Osaka shrine »] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225041706/http://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/AJ201811150004.html |date=2020-02-25 }}, ''The Asahi Shimbun'', 15 novembre 2018.</ref>
Every third Sunday in January, a rite inspired by [[Japanese mythology]] is performed there. It tells the story of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of Wind and Sea, and the dragon [[:en:Yamata-no-Orochi|Yamata-no-Orochi]] . In 2001, this ritual was officially recognized as an element of the intangible heritage of the city of Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Namba Yasaka Shrine|url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/page/nanbayasakajinja|access-date=18 juillet 2019|website=osaka-info.jp|language=en}}.</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Nanba yasaka shrine exterior.jpg|View of the complex from outside File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine File:Nanba yasaka shrine 02.JPG|Look at the mouth from afar File:Tabemas.jpg|Side view of the head File:Interior of the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Inside the Lion's Head Theater at Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Roof of the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja in Osaka.jpg|View of the roof File:Komainu at the Namba Yasaka Jinja.jpg|A Komainu guarding Namba Yasaka Jinja File:Chōzuya at Namba-Yasaka-Jinja, Osaka.jpg|{{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|WD=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya|1=Chōzuya}} File:Lanterns on the Haiden at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka.jpg|Lanterns at the Haiden File:Namba-yasaka-jinja sasayama jinja.jpg|A subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja two subshrines.jpg|Two subshrines File:Namba-yasaka-jinja Torii to subshrine.jpg|Torii of a subshrine File:Namba-yasaka-jinja stele.jpg|Stone carving File:Omikuji at Namba Yasaka Jinja, Osaka closeup.jpg|Omikuji </gallery>
